definition: The Equity Risk Premium is compressed by any methodology, but its precise level is model-dependent with a range of ~500bp across approaches (Fed Model ~0.5% to survey-based ~5.5%). This renders specific compression claims unfalsifiable without specifying the model. Directionally, ERP is below its post-1990 median by any methodology.
evidence: Current estimates cluster around 1.0-3.0% depending on methodology
500bp range across Fed Model, Damodaran DDM, CAPE-inverted, survey, and historical realized approaches
Cap-weighted vs equal-weighted ERP divergence of ~200bp directly observable
5-10 year forward return relationship robust per Campbell-Shiller
1 out of 5 episodes delivered >3% real annualized from similar starting ERP
macro_strategist_02 internal contradiction (2.5-3.0% headline vs 0.2-0.5% calculated)
open_questions: Which ERP methodology is most appropriate for current market structure
Whether cap-weighted or equal-weighted ERP is the relevant metric (depends on AI concentration being structural vs cyclical)
Whether passive flow mechanics have structurally altered the ERP
